title: callirhoe triangulata
intro: Callirhoe triangulata, also known as the white spider flower or three-angled spider flower, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Callirhoe triangulata include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional folk medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
